Question:
We will plan to do a logistic regression from NHANES data from 2011-2020. We will exclude participants whose age at screening is less than 18 years, missing values in gender, race/Hispanic origin, urine albumin (mg/L), or urine creatinine (mg/dL), serum creatinine (mg/dl), serum total folate (nmol/l), red blood cell folate (nmol/l).
We will include participants who had diabetes, defined as meeting one of the criteria: having self-reported diabetes, fasting plasma glucose >= 7.0 mmol/L, glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >=6.5 , or random glucose level >= 11.1 mmol/L
The main predictor is red blood cell folate (nmol/l), categorized in quintiles(%) QN1: ≤20, QN2: >20 and ≤40, QN3: >40 and ≤60, QN4: >60 and ≤80, QN5: >80.
The outcome of interest is the presence of kidney disease, defined as either an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or a urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) (mg/g) of 30 mg/g or greater. 
eGFR can be calculated using gender, serum creatinine (mg/dl), age at screening, race/Hispanic origin for 4 different cases: 
1. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159
2. if gender is female and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018
3. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159
4. if gender is male and race/Hispanic origin is not Non-Hispanic Black : 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)
urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) is calculated as 100*(urine albumin mg/L / urine creatinine mg/dL).
Adjust the analysis for age at screening, gender, and race/Hispanic origin.
Structured Question:
Analysis Type: logistic regression

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2020

Inclusion Criteria:
- diabetes defined as meeting one of the criteria:
  having self-reported diabetes OR fasting plasma glucose >= 7.0 mmol/L OR glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) (%) >= 6.5 OR random glucose level >= 11.1 mmol/L

Exclusion Criteria:
- age at screening < 18
- missing gender
- missing race/Hispanic origin
- missing urine albumin (mg/L)
- missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
- missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
- missing serum total folate (nmol/l)
- missing red blood cell folate (nmol/l)

Covariates:
- age at screening
- gender
- race/Hispanic origin

Predictor:
- red blood cell folate (nmol/l) categorized in quintiles(%):
  QN1: ≤20, QN2: >20 and ≤40, QN3: >40 and ≤60, QN4: >60 and ≤80, QN5: >80

Outcome:
- presence of kidney disease = 1 if (1) or (2) is met; 0 otherwise
  (1) eGFR < 60 mL/min/1.73m^2, where eGFR is calculated for 4 cases:
    If gender = female and race/Hispanic origin = Non-Hispanic Black:
      eGFR = 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018 * 1.159
    If gender = female and race/Hispanic origin ≠ Non-Hispanic Black:
      eGFR = 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -0.329 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.7, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.018
    If gender = male and race/Hispanic origin = Non-Hispanic Black:
      eGFR = 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening) * 1.159
    If gender = male and race/Hispanic origin ≠ Non-Hispanic Black:
      eGFR = 141 * min(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -0.411 * max(serum creatinine (mg/dl) / 0.9, 1) ** -1.209 * (0.993 ** age at screening)
or
  (2) urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) >= 30, where:
    urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (mg/g) = 100 * (urine albumin (mg/L) / urine creatinine (mg/dL))

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "self-reported diabetes"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.p_diq
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_i;

CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes ON temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes (respondent_sequence_number);

-- ALL UNIQUE PATIENT IDs
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_all_ids;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_all_ids AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_fasting_glucose_mmol_l
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_glycohemoglobin
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_glucose_serum_mmol_l
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_gender
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_race_hispanic_origin
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_serum_total_folate_nmol_l
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_rbc_folate_nmol_l;

-- Add index to the main ID table for the final join
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_all_ids_respondent_sequence_number ON temp_all_ids (respondent_sequence_number);

-- FINAL MASTER TABLE
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS final_master_table;
CREATE TABLE final_master_table AS
SELECT
    a.respondent_sequence_number,
    t0."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes",
    t1."fasting_glucose_mmol_l",
    t2."glycohemoglobin",
    t3."glucose_serum_mmol_l",
    t4."age_in_years_at_screening",
    t5."gender",
    t6."race_hispanic_origin",
    t7."albumin_urine_mg_l",
    t8."creatinine_urine_mg_dl",
    t9."creatinine_mg_dl",
    t10."serum_total_folate_nmol_l",
    t11."rbc_folate_nmol_l"
FROM temp_all_ids a
LEFT JOIN temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes t0 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t0.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_fasting_glucose_mmol_l t1 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t1.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_glycohemoglobin t2 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t2.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_glucose_serum_mmol_l t3 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t3.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening t4 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t4.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_gender t5 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t5.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_race_hispanic_origin t6 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t6.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_albumin_urine_mg_l t7 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t7.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_creatinine_urine_mg_dl t8 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t8.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl t9 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t9.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_serum_total_folate_nmol_l t10 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t10.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_rbc_folate_nmol_l t11 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t11.respondent_sequence_number;

CREATE INDEX idx_final_master_respondent_sequence_number    
    ON final_master_table (respondent_sequence_number);
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Include patients with diabetes (self-reported, FPG>=7.0, HbA1c>=6.5, or random glucose>=11.1)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE ("doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Yes')
   OR ("fasting_glucose_mmol_l" >= 7.0)
   OR ("glycohemoglobin" >= 6.5)
   OR ("glucose_serum_mmol_l" >= 11.1);

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude patients with age at screening < 18
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 18;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude patients with missing gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."gender" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude patients with missing race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."race_hispanic_origin" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude patients with missing urine albumin (mg/L)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_urine_mg_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude patients with missing urine creatinine (mg/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.6: Exclude patients with missing serum creatinine (mg/dl)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step6 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."creatinine_mg_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.7: Exclude patients with missing serum total folate (nmol/l)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step7 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step6 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."serum_total_folate_nmol_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.8: Exclude patients with missing red blood cell folate (nmol/l)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step8 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step7 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."rbc_folate_nmol_l" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step8;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Age at screening
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Gender
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Red blood cell folate - raw values
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_rbc_folate_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number", f."rbc_folate_nmol_l"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quintile cutoffs for red blood cell folate within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_rbc_folate_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.20) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "rbc_folate_nmol_l") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.40) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "rbc_folate_nmol_l") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.60) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "rbc_folate_nmol_l") AS q3,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.80) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "rbc_folate_nmol_l") AS q4
FROM temp_rbc_folate_raw;

-- Assign red blood cell folate quintile groups
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_rbc_folate_quintiles AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN "rbc_folate_nmol_l" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN "rbc_folate_nmol_l" <= (SELECT q1 FROM temp_rbc_folate_cutoffs) THEN 'QN1'
    WHEN "rbc_folate_nmol_l" <= (SELECT q2 FROM temp_rbc_folate_cutoffs) THEN 'QN2'
    WHEN "rbc_folate_nmol_l" <= (SELECT q3 FROM temp_rbc_folate_cutoffs) THEN 'QN3'
    WHEN "rbc_folate_nmol_l" <= (SELECT q4 FROM temp_rbc_folate_cutoffs) THEN 'QN4'
    ELSE 'QN5'
  END AS "red_blood_cell_folate_nmol_l"
FROM temp_rbc_folate_raw;

-- 4.5 Outcome: Presence of kidney disease
-- Calculate eGFR based on gender, race, age, and serum creatinine
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * 
      POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * 
      POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018 * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -0.329) * 
      POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7, 1), -1.209) * 
      POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.018
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * 
      POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * 
      POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.159
    WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."race_hispanic_origin" != 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN
      141 * POWER(LEAST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -0.411) * 
      POWER(GREATEST(f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9, 1), -1.209) * 
      POWER(0.993, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "egfr"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Calculate urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_uacr AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       (100.0 * f."albumin_urine_mg_l" / f."creatinine_urine_mg_dl") AS "uacr"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Determine presence of kidney disease
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_kidney_disease AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN e."egfr" < 60 OR u."uacr" >= 30 THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS "presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_egfr e ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = e."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_uacr u ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = u."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT 
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  a."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening",
  g."gender",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  rbc."red_blood_cell_folate_nmol_l",
  kd."presence_of_kidney_disease"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_rbc_folate_quintiles rbc ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = rbc."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_kidney_disease kd ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = kd."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(broom))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tibble))

    identifier_vars <- c("respondent_sequence_number")
    outcome_vars <- c("presence_of_kidney_disease")
    
    cohort <- df


    outcome_roles <- c()
    outcome_roles["presence_of_kidney_disease"] <- "outcome"


    summarize_variable <- function(column, name) {
    n_unique <- length(unique(na.omit(column)))
    na_count <- sum(is.na(column))

    if (name %in% identifier_vars) {
        category <- "identifier"
        summary_info <- paste0("Total rows = ", nrow(cohort))

    } else if (name %in% outcome_vars) {
        # Assign its special role (e.g., "outcome", "mediator")
        category <- ifelse(!is.na(outcome_roles[name]), outcome_roles[name], "outcome")

        # Generate a summary based on its underlying data type
        underlying_type <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (underlying_type == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (underlying_type %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }

    

    } else {
        # This block handles regular predictors
        category <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (category == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (category %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }
    }

    data.frame(
        variable   = name,
        category   = category,
        n_unique   = n_unique,
        na_count   = na_count,
        summary    = summary_info,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    }

    report <- bind_rows(
    lapply(names(cohort), function(col_name) {
        summarize_variable(cohort[[col_name]], col_name)
    })
    )

    identifier_vars  <- report$variable[report$category == "identifier"]
    cat("Identifier Variables:\n");   print(identifier_vars);   cat("\n")

    

    binary_vars      <- report$variable[report$category == "binary"]
    cat("Binary Variables:\n");       print(binary_vars);       cat("\n")
    print(binary_vars)

    categorical_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "categorical"]
    cat("Categorical Variables:\n");  print(categorical_vars);  cat("\n")
    print(categorical_vars)

    numerical_vars   <- report$variable[report$category == "numerical"]
    cat("Numerical Variables:\n");    print(numerical_vars);    cat("\n")
    print(numerical_vars)

    
    

    outcome_var     <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("outcome")]
    cat("Outcome Variables:\n");      print(outcome_var);      cat("\n")
    

    
        
    
    
    # Identify predictors and factor candidates
    predictor_vars <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary", "numerical")]
    factor_candidates <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary")]

    # Convert binary/categorical variables to factor if not already
    for (var in factor_candidates) {
    if (!is.factor(df[[var]])) {
        df[[var]] <- as.factor(df[[var]])
    }
    }

    # Initialize reference_levels table
    reference_levels <- tibble(variable = character(), reference_level = character())

    # Clean, trim, and set reference levels for all factor variables
    for (col in factor_candidates) {
    df[[col]] <- factor(trimws(as.character(df[[col]])))
    levels_vec <- levels(df[[col]])

    # Try to find preferred reference
    preferred_order <- c("healthy", "none", "control", "reference", "ref", "normal","Q1", "low", "0", "1")
    matches <- tolower(levels_vec)  # lower case to standardize
    ref_level <- NA  # default

    for (keyword in preferred_order) {
    idx <- which(grepl(keyword, matches, ignore.case = TRUE))
    if (length(idx) > 0) {
        ref_level <- levels_vec[idx[1]]  # pick the first match for that keyword
        break  # stop at the highest-priority match
    }
    }


    # If none found, fall back to most common level
    if (is.na(ref_level)) {
        level_counts <- table(df[[col]])
        ref_level <- names(level_counts)[which.max(level_counts)]
    }

    # Set reference level
    df[[col]] <- relevel(df[[col]], ref = ref_level)

    # Record reference level
    reference_levels <- bind_rows(
        reference_levels,
        tibble(variable = col, reference_level = ref_level)
    )
    }
    
        
    
    x_raw <- df[[outcome_var]]
    x_chr <- trimws(tolower(as.character(x_raw)))

    none_tokens <- c("none", "nan", "na", "", "null", "nil", "missing")
    x_chr[x_chr %in% none_tokens] <- NA_character_

    y <- rep(NA_integer_, length(x_chr))

    y[x_chr %in% c("yes", "y", "true", "t")]  <- 1L
    y[x_chr %in% c("no",  "n", "false", "f")] <- 0L

    x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x_chr))
    y[is.na(y) & !is.na(x_num) & x_num == 1] <- 1L
    y[is.na(y) & !is.na(x_num) & x_num == 0] <- 0L

    idx <- is.na(y) & !is.na(x_chr)
    if (any(idx)) {
    vals <- sort(unique(x_chr[idx]))

    if (length(vals) != 2) {
        stop(sprintf(
        "Outcome '%s' must have exactly 2 non-missing classes after excluding none. Found: %s",
        outcome_var, paste(vals, collapse = ", ")
        ))
    }

    tab <- table(x_chr[idx])
    minority <- names(tab)[which.min(tab)]
    majority <- setdiff(vals, minority)

    cat(sprintf(
        "Outcome '%s' recoding: 1 = %s (minority)  0 = %s (majority)",
        outcome_var, minority, majority
    ))

    y[idx] <- as.integer(x_chr[idx] == minority)
    }

    df[[outcome_var]] <- y

    # Must contain both 0 and 1 somewhere (plus NA allowed)
    u <- sort(unique(df[[outcome_var]][!is.na(df[[outcome_var]])]))
    if (!(length(u) == 2 && all(u == c(0, 1)))) {
    stop(sprintf(
        "After recoding, '%s' must contain both 0 and 1 (plus NA for none). Found: %s",
        outcome_var,
        ifelse(length(u) == 0, "<no non-missing values>", paste(u, collapse = ", "))
    ))
    }

    formula <- as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ ", paste(predictor_vars, collapse = " + ")))
                
    
        
    
    model <- glm(formula,
                data = df,
                family =  binomial(link = "logit"))
            
    
    result_table <- tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
        select(term, estimate, p.value, conf.low, conf.high)
